name: F.E. Warren AFB
profile: Military-friendly F.E. Warren AFB housing snapshot anchored to Cheyenne, Laramie County, and southeastern Wyoming, built for PCSUnited base-level market overviews.

market_metrics.market_type_summary: Stable High Plains military market with moderate buyer leverage, manageable commute pressure, and weather-sensitive ownership considerations

mortgage_assumptions.defaults_note: Based on March 2026 Wyoming VA loan planning assumptions.

notes: Wyoming property taxes can be comparatively manageable, but wind, hail, snow, roof condition, insulation, and heating costs should be included in all-in ownership comparisons.
Cheyenne can offer strong practical military-family value, but buyers should still review neighborhood, school, commute, and property condition carefully.
Front Range proximity can create regional demand pressure while still offering lower cost than many Colorado markets.

avg_home_mortgage_monthly.notes: VA loan planning estimate for practical F.E. Warren / Cheyenne-area housing, including Wyoming tax and insurance assumptions.

crime_status.installation: Extremely safe; gated military installation with controlled access.
crime_status.metro_cheyenne_laramie_county: Cheyenne is generally manageable by military-market standards, but safety, property condition, school zones, and resale quality still vary by exact neighborhood and address.

school_quality.on_base: No full on-base K-12 school system should be assumed; families should verify current school options and attendance boundaries by exact housing address.
school_quality.off_base: Laramie County School District zones vary by address. Families should compare Cheyenne, north/northeast corridors, west-side neighborhoods, and rural-edge options by exact school assignment.

rental_vacancy.notes: Rental inventory is influenced by F.E. Warren AFB, state government, logistics, Front Range movement, and limited family-size single-family rental supply.

rental_metrics.notes: Planning estimate for 3-bedroom military-family rentals in the Cheyenne / F.E. Warren area.

military_lifestyle_fit.description: Historic missile-wing base with strong military identity, low commute stress, stable Cheyenne market, and practical access to Wyoming and Colorado Front Range amenities.

financial_brief.affordability_summary: F.E. Warren offers a practical ownership lane because taxes are manageable and commute pressure is low, but purchase prices, wind/hail insurance, winter utilities, and property condition should still be tested.
financial_brief.buyer_opportunity: Strongest for disciplined VA buyers comparing Cheyenne neighborhoods, north/northeast corridors, west Cheyenne, and rural-edge options by commute, schools, weather exposure, and all-in payment.

market_bluf.bluf_summary: F.E. Warren gives military families a stable Cheyenne housing lane with manageable commute and taxes, but the best decision depends on wind, winter, property condition, school zones, and assignment length.

summary_points: F.E. Warren AFB sits inside a stable Cheyenne housing market with lower commute stress than many military metros.
Cheyenne has meaningful Front Range influence, but still remains more practical than many Colorado-side markets.
Low property taxes help ownership math, but insurance, wind, hail, snow, heating, and property condition still matter.
The strongest housing choices balance commute, schools, neighborhood quality, roof condition, insulation, and resale logic.
F.E. Warren works best for disciplined buyers who want stability and avoid overpaying for a house that needs major weather-related repairs.
opportunities: Manageable commute patterns can improve daily quality of life.
Lower property taxes support practical ownership math.
Stable military, state government, logistics, and regional demand support housing relevance.
VA buyers may find a strong payment-to-lifestyle balance if property condition is clean.
Cheyenne offers access to Denver/Fort Collins amenities without living inside a more expensive Colorado market.
risks: Wind, hail, snow, and roof condition can materially affect ownership cost.
Older homes may require insulation, windows, HVAC, roof, plumbing, or electrical review.
Fast-moving well-priced inventory can pressure buyers to move quickly.
School zones and neighborhood quality should be verified by exact address.
Short assignments may favor renting or on-base housing if resale timing is uncertain.
buyer_notes: F.E. Warren buyers should compare Cheyenne neighborhoods by commute, school zones, wind exposure, property age, and all-in payment.
Do not judge affordability by mortgage alone; heating, insurance, roof condition, and weather readiness matter.
Well-maintained homes with newer roof, windows, insulation, and HVAC may be worth more than cheaper homes with deferred maintenance.
Rural-edge properties can offer space but may require more due diligence on utilities, road access, snow, and resale.
This market rewards buyers who preserve residual income and avoid rushing into weak-condition inventory.
seller_notes: Sellers should position homes around commute, VA readiness, roof condition, winter readiness, insulation, and low-maintenance ownership.
Move-in-ready homes with updated roof, HVAC, windows, and clear inspection confidence can stand out.
Military buyers respond well to practical monthly value and PCS-friendly timing.
Homes in strong Cheyenne corridors should emphasize F.E. Warren access, school-zone confidence, and weather-ready maintenance history.
investor_angles: Military, state government, logistics, and Front Range-related demand can support rental relevance.
Family-size rentals near practical commute corridors may appeal to military families avoiding purchase risk.
Investors should stress-test wind/hail insurance, roof life, winter utilities, vacancy, and maintenance reserves.
Clean single-family homes with strong condition and easy commute may outperform cheaper but deferred-maintenance inventory.
buyer_guidance: Use all-in monthly cost as the primary decision filter.
Compare Cheyenne neighborhoods by commute, school zone, safety, property condition, wind exposure, and resale appeal.
Verify roof age, hail history, insulation, windows, HVAC, heating costs, drainage, and utility expectations before finalizing a purchase budget.
Avoid choosing only by lowest price if the home has major weather-related maintenance risk.
Use PCSUnited calculators to compare rent, buy, residual income, winter utility pressure, commute risk, and assignment-length exposure.
seller_guidance: Price against current buyer payment sensitivity and local inventory.
Highlight commute logic, VA readiness, roof condition, winter readiness, insulation, and low-maintenance features.
Homes with updated roof, HVAC, windows, and clean inspection history should be positioned clearly.
Military buyers need practical value and confidence in monthly cost, not generic listing language.
landlord_notes: Military renter demand can support stable occupancy near F.E. Warren AFB.
Single-family rentals in good school and commute corridors may appeal to military families avoiding purchase risk.
Investors should model wind/hail insurance, roof wear, winter maintenance, vacancy, and turnover conservatively.
Clean, functional homes near base, services, and schools may outperform cheaper but weaker-condition inventory.

market.market_type_summary: Stable Cheyenne military market with moderate buyer leverage and weather-sensitive ownership costs

base_profile.display_name: Francis E. Warren Air Force Base
base_profile.market_label: Cheyenne / F.E. Warren Military Housing Market
base_profile.installation_type: Missile Wing / Nuclear Deterrence / Global Strike Base
base_profile.host_or_major_command: 90th Missile Wing / Air Force Global Strike Command
base_profile.primary_mission_summary: F.E. Warren AFB is a major Air Force Global Strike Command installation in Cheyenne supporting intercontinental ballistic missile operations, nuclear deterrence, security forces, maintenance, mission support, and strategic readiness across the northern High Plains.
base_profile.pcs_personality: High Plains, mission-focused, wind-weather-sensitive, commute-light, family-relevant, stable, and strongly tied to Cheyenne, Laramie County, and Front Range-adjacent housing decisions.
base_profile.base_bluf: F.E. Warren is not just a Cheyenne housing decision. It is a BAH, wind, hail, winter, roof-condition, school-zone, property-condition, and assignment-length decision. The market can work well, but the home must be weather-ready and financially disciplined.

user_positioning.military_family_bluf: Prioritize school-zone verification, commute simplicity, weather-ready condition, roof age, heating cost, BAH fit, and assignment length before signing a lease or purchase contract.
user_positioning.single_airman_bluf: Prioritize short commute, rent control, roommate flexibility, access to daily services, and avoiding unnecessary overbuying if the assignment timeline is short.
user_positioning.realtor_bluf: The strongest F.E. Warren housing conversations are about weather-ready condition, VA readiness, school-zone confidence, commute simplicity, and tax-aware payment math.
user_positioning.investor_bluf: Rental demand near F.E. Warren is influenced by military turnover, state government, logistics, Front Range spillover, and the need for clean weather-ready homes in practical commute corridors.

on_base_housing.map_zone: housing support area / family housing communities
best_for: Families wanting to reduce commute uncertainty
Users who want predictable access to base services
watchouts: Availability and waitlists can change
On-base housing may capture BAH differently than off-base renting
Pet policies and breed restrictions should be verified
School assignment must be verified by exact address
Off-base options in Cheyenne and Laramie County should be compared carefully
Wind, hail, winter conditions, roof age, heating, and insulation should be modeled for off-base purchase scenarios
on_base_housing.pcsu_strategy_note: Compare on-base housing against off-base rent and mortgage options. Around F.E. Warren, the best choice depends on rank, dependents, BAH, debt, commute tolerance, school priorities, weather readiness, roof condition, pets, and expected assignment length.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: North Cheyenne
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Popular practical corridor with access to services, suburban feel, and manageable commute to F.E. Warren.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 10-25 minutes depending on exact address, gate, and traffic
likely_gate_logic: Gate 2 depending on route and current access rules
watchouts: Prices can be higher in stronger pockets
School assignment should be verified by exact address
Wind and roof condition should be checked carefully
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Strong first-look area for families who want practicality, services, and manageable commute.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Northeast Cheyenne
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Growth-oriented corridor with newer housing options and practical access to the base and city services.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 15-30 minutes depending on exact address and gate
watchouts: Newer subdivisions may include HOA costs
Wind exposure and snow management should be considered
Pricing can stretch some BAH profiles
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Good family and lower-maintenance candidate when the payment fits.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: West Cheyenne
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Practical base-access area with potentially shorter commute and a mix of older and newer inventory.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 5-20 minutes depending on exact address and gate
watchouts: Property age and condition vary
Roof, insulation, windows, and HVAC should be inspected carefully
Neighborhood and school fit should be verified
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Strong practical option for commute-first users if the exact property condition is solid.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Central Cheyenne Selected Pockets
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Close-in Cheyenne option with access to downtown services and older-home character, but condition and resale screening matter.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 5-20 minutes depending on exact address and gate
watchouts: Older homes may need stronger inspection
Parking, insulation, windows, roof, and plumbing should be reviewed
School and resale appeal vary by pocket
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Use selectively. Central Cheyenne can work well, but the property must be condition-strong.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Ranchettes
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Larger-lot and rural-edge option north of Cheyenne with space and privacy, but more weather, utility, and commute diligence.
best_for: Experienced buyers comfortable with rural details
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 20-40+ minutes depending on exact address, road conditions, and gate
watchouts: Wind exposure can be significant
Septic, well, propane, road access, snow drifting, and utility details should be verified
Resale market may be more specialized
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Best for users who intentionally want space and understand rural-edge ownership responsibilities.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Fox Farm-College / South Cheyenne
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Practical affordability corridor with access to Cheyenne services and manageable commute.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 10-25 minutes depending on exact address and gate
watchouts: Neighborhood and property condition vary
School assignment should be verified
Older homes may require maintenance reserves
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Useful affordability candidate when the exact property and neighborhood fit are strong.

commute_intelligence.overall_commute_posture: Generally favorable because Cheyenne is compact and traffic is manageable, but wind, snow, and winter road conditions still matter.
primary_commute_risks: Gate selection based on duty location
Choosing by purchase price instead of property condition
Rural utilities and road access for outer areas
planning_guidance: Test the commute during expected report-time windows.
Confirm which gate best supports the user’s duty location.
Compare Cheyenne neighborhoods by route reliability, schools, condition, weather exposure, and resale.
Include winter utilities, roof/hail risk, and wind exposure in the housing decision.
Use commute plus all-in housing cost as the core PCSUnited decision screen.
commute_intelligence.pcsu_bluf: F.E. Warren commute risk is usually low, but High Plains weather, wind, snow, and property condition can change the real housing decision.

bah_market_reality.buying_power_summary: BAH can support buying for many F.E. Warren households when the target price, property condition, and residual income are controlled.
bah_market_reality.renting_summary: Renting remains practical for short assignments or users still learning Cheyenne, but ownership can be attractive when the property is weather-ready and resale-friendly.
bah_market_reality.pcsu_strategy_note: F.E. Warren should be modeled as a favorable but weather-sensitive market. PCSUnited should test rank, BAH, debt, savings, credit score, family size, commute tolerance, weather-readiness, property condition, and assignment length before recommending buy versus rent.

strengths: Access to Cheyenne services and Front Range amenities
watchouts: Wind and winter weather are major lifestyle factors
School assignments must be verified by exact address
Older homes may need roof/HVAC/insulation/window review
Rural-edge homes require utility and road-access diligence
Short assignments require careful rent-versus-buy analysis
family_readiness.pcsu_family_bluf: F.E. Warren can be a strong family PCS location when the household chooses a weather-ready home, verifies schools, protects residual income, and avoids weak-condition properties.

arrival_checklist: Verify current gate hours and visitor access procedures through official F.E. Warren AFB sources.
Contact the F.E. Warren Housing Office early for current housing conditions, eligibility, and wait-time guidance.
Confirm whether on-base housing or off-base housing better fits rank, dependents, BAH, pets, school needs, and expected assignment length.
Compare North Cheyenne, Northeast Cheyenne, West Cheyenne, Central Cheyenne, Ranchettes, and South Cheyenne using commute, schools, safety, condition, and all-in monthly cost.
Check roof age, hail history, insulation, windows, HVAC, heating cost, drainage, utilities, and wind exposure before finalizing a purchase budget.
Verify school zones by exact property address.
Use PCSUnited calculators to compare rent, buy, residual income, winter utility pressure, commute risk, and assignment-length exposure before committing.
pcs_watchouts: Do not assume low property taxes mean the home is automatically affordable.
Do not ignore wind, hail, roof age, insulation, or winter heating cost.
Do not choose by distance alone; property condition and school zone matter.
Verify rural utilities and road access before buying outside town.
Short assignments may favor renting or on-base housing if resale risk is high.
A fast-moving local market should not override inspection discipline.

realtor_intelligence.best_client_positioning: Lead with weather-ready condition, VA readiness, school-zone confidence, commute simplicity, and all-in monthly cost. F.E. Warren clients need practical Cheyenne housing guidance with High Plains risk awareness.
strong_listing_angles: Short commute to F.E. Warren AFB
Updated roof, HVAC, windows, insulation, and exterior condition
buyer_consultation_angles: Rent versus buy based on assignment length
All-in payment including taxes, insurance, heating, utilities, and maintenance
Roof, hail, insulation, and weather-readiness review
Future resale appeal to military and Cheyenne buyers
investor_consultation_angles: State government and regional workforce demand
realtor_intelligence.pcsu_realtor_bluf: A strong F.E. Warren realtor should sound like a weather-risk advisor, VA-condition filter, and military affordability strategist — not just a local salesperson.

notes: Market and housing values are PCSUnited planning estimates and should be refreshed periodically.
Gate, visitor, service, medical, and housing office details should be verified against official installation sources before publication or use in routing.
Placeholder map image is used until a dedicated F.E. Warren base orientation map is created.
Cheyenne-area pricing and commute experience can vary by city pocket, rural-edge location, property condition, and weather exposure.

source_notes: Use official F.E. Warren AFB and Military OneSource sources for housing, gate, visitor, newcomer, and installation guidance.
Use official Air Force Housing, F.E. Warren Housing Office, and privatized housing sources for on-base housing details.
Use DeCA, AAFES, and TRICARE sources for commissary, exchange, and medical details.
Use Zillow-style indexes, local MLS, rental aggregators, HUD, and Census-style demographic sources for housing and market refreshes.
base_profile.disclaimer: PCSUnited base profiles are planning tools only. They are not official DoD, Air Force, installation, legal, financial, real estate, lending, insurance, school-zone, or navigation guidance. Users should verify gate access, housing rules, school zones, medical services, visitor procedures, market prices, insurance estimates, commute conditions, winter-road conditions, utility costs, heating systems, roof condition, and property condition through official and professional sources before making decisions.

demographics.provider: US Census Bureau / Cheyenne and Laramie County planning estimates

rent.provider: Local rental listing estimates / Cheyenne-area rental observations

base_profile.provider: PCSUnited planning profile using public-facing installation, housing, and market research structure

parser_notes: Built in the PCSUnited city/base JSON pattern used by Lackland, Andrews, Barksdale, Beale, Cannon, Charleston, Davis-Monthan, Dover, Dyess, Eglin, and Elmendorf.
All major PCSUnited fields included and adapted for F.E. Warren AFB.
Compatible with PCS Snapshot, Base Demographics Shell, Base SaaS, and TOR parsing.
Preserves duplicate value aliases for avg_home_value, average_home_value, avgHome, and city_avg_home.
Preserves duplicate median price aliases for median_list_price, median_sold_price, and median_sale_price.
Preserves by_bedroom entries for 2, 3, 4, and 5-bedroom Market Insight compatibility.
